### Post by illbashu on 2009-09-26
Well I boot up my computer Grub isn't picking up on the windows partition. Is there a fix for this?

Here is my Grub config file

```

#
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE
#
# It is automatically generated by /usr/sbin/grub-mkconfig using templates
# from /etc/grub.d and settings from /etc/default/grub
#

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/00_header ###
load_env
set default=0
if [ ${prev_saved_entry} ]; then
  saved_entry=${prev_saved_entry}
  save_env saved_entry
  prev_saved_entry=
  save_env prev_saved_entry
fi
insmod ext2
set root=(hd0,5)
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set dfb17e87-91b6-4c9f-b2d1-509e5f36b8af
if loadfont /usr/share/grub/unicode.pf2 ; then
  set gfxmode=640x480
  insmod gfxterm
  insmod vbe
  if terminal_output gfxterm ; then true ; else
    # For backward compatibility with versions of terminal.mod that don't
    # understand terminal_output
    terminal gfxterm
  fi
fi
set timeout=10
### END /etc/grub.d/00_header ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###
set menu_color_normal=white/black
set menu_color_highlight=black/white
### END /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###
menuentry "Ubuntu, Linux 2.6.31-10-generic" {
	set quiet=1
	insmod ext2
	set root=(hd1,6)
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 0eadc933-8a02-419a-86e7-040f9d794c0c
	linux	/vmlinuz-2.6.31-10-generic root=UUID=dfb17e87-91b6-4c9f-b2d1-509e5f36b8af ro   quiet splash
	initrd	/initrd.img-2.6.31-10-generic
}
menuentry "Ubuntu, Linux 2.6.31-10-generic (recovery mode)" {
	insmod ext2
	set root=(hd1,6)
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 0eadc933-8a02-419a-86e7-040f9d794c0c
	linux	/vmlinuz-2.6.31-10-generic root=UUID=dfb17e87-91b6-4c9f-b2d1-509e5f36b8af ro single 
	initrd	/initrd.img-2.6.31-10-generic
}
### END /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+)" {
	linux16	/memtest86+.bin
}
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+, serial console 115200)" {
	linux16	/memtest86+.bin console=ttyS0,115200n8
}
### END /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###
### END /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###
# This file provides an easy way to add custom menu entries.  Simply type the
# menu entries you want to add after this comment.  Be careful not to change
# the 'exec tail' line above.
### END /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###

# This is a divider, added to separate the menu items below from the Debian
# ones.
title		Other operating systems:
root

```

---

### Post by QIII on 2009-09-26
```
sudo apt-get install os-prober
```

(You may get a message that you have the newest installed already.  Just move on.)

```
sudo os-prober
```

```
sudo update-grub2
```

I sure hope this becomes an automatic part of the installation when the official release comes out.

---

### Post by illbashu on 2009-09-26
> **QIII said:**
> ```
sudo apt-get install os-prober
```

(You may get a message that you have the newest installed already.  Just move on.)

```
sudo os-prober
```

```
sudo update-grub2
```

I sure hope this becomes an automatic part of the installation when the official release comes out.

Holy ****, that worked like a charm. :D +1 Rep, that Application is awesome!

Edit: WTF? The rep system is gone? 0_o
